---
title: "Q1: What is a data set?"
description: "A data set is a collection of observations."
date: 2026-07-10
categories: [beginner]
---

## Answer

A data set is a collection of observations.
Here is an example of a data set that contains names, college majors, and final exam scores of five students in a statistics course -
| **Observation** | **Name** | **Major**  | **Score** |
|-----------------|----------|------------|-----------|
| 1               | Andrew   | Statistics | 91        |
| 2               | Becky    | Marketing  | 85        |
| 3               | Charlie  | Psychology | 87        |
| 4               | Cynthia  | Statistics | 89        |
| 5               | Dexter   | Marketing  | 92        |

## Prerequisites
Nothing yet — this is Q1, the foundation stone.

## Terms in this entry

- **term** — working definition (also added to the [Glossary](../../glossary.qmd))
